ref: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
text: Ka yɛli u, ni, “I yi ti gbaani tiŋŋa n‑gyeem ma Naawunni gyeeŋŋu, n nin zaŋŋi anŋɔ zaa n‑ti i.”
translated: He said to him, "You have been faithful in a very small matter, I will put you in charge of ten cities."

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Dinŋɔ daa kyɛ ka anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa yɛli seem la niŋi yɛlimaŋni, ni#:-
translated: The day that the prophet Isaiah spoke those words to the people, he said:-

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Tɔ. Yisa ŋɔn daa nya niriba maa, u daa kyaŋŋi nti du zoori la ni. U daa zinni bɛni ka u nyaandɔliba daa kyɛ u saani na.
translated: No. Jesus did not have children, he was not married at all. He died when his followers were still with him.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Zugsuŋŋu bɛ ni n‑ti ninvugseeba ban mari sufu yinni, dama ba nin ti nya Naawunni.
translated: The leaders and the young people did not agree on that matter, because they did not know God.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di pɔ noori halli beela. Di pɔ arizana, dama Naawunni naam zizii nla.
translated: But when they came, they found only empty shells. They were amazed, because God's name is great.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya di teesi, ni, n kyɛnni na ni n ti sa'am Musa zaaligu ni anabidima la bulisigu yɛla. Ay! N bu kyɛ na ni n ti sa'am a ni, amaa ni n ti kyɛ ka a niŋi yɛlimaŋni.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "Go and show yourselves to the priests, and offer the gift that Moses commanded as a testimony to them. Yes! I am telling you to go, but you will be healed as you go."

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, ŋɔn suuri yisigi n‑niŋi u mabiiya, u bɛ la sariya karibu ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin bunkyɔ'aŋŋu wa!’, ba nin boosi u boosa gyintooriba duu maa ni. Ka ŋɔn mun ti laa n‑yɛli u mabiiya, ‘Nyin gyɛrigu wa!’, u nin di wahala bugum ni.
translated: But if you do not do this, and you continue to sin against the children of Israel, you will be breaking the law. And if you do not keep your promise to the children of Israel, saying 'We will help you!', they will drive you away from their land. And if you do not keep your promise to the children of Israel, saying 'We are with you!', you will be in great trouble.

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Amaa man yɛtti ya wa, ni, yan di zabi ni ya ta'ai ya taalli daana tuma; faasee ka sɔ yi lɔbi i tampɛkka, nyin ŋmaligma kanla gba m‑paasi u.
translated: But if you do not do this, then you have sinned against the Lord your God; and be sure your sin will find you out.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: Sɔ yi ti ya taasi i kootu ni n‑deaai i bunyeerigu, ka laa m‑boori i kyinkyinni m‑paasi, nyin di mɔŋŋi u.
translated: If you want to put your hand in a pot of hot food, first test the heat with your finger.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: I yi ya puusi Naawunni, di niŋi hoo birikɔnsi dima: ba boori ba zɛ saazugu m‑puusiri gyeeŋŋu duri ni, ni sɔkyira ni, ni niriba ti nya ba. Yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, ba pun deaai ba laara zaa n‑naai.
translated: They went to the Lord, and they saw many miracles there: they saw the blind receive sight and the lame walk and lepers be cleansed. In the evening when the sun was setting, they brought all the sick to him.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Dinŋɔ ka ya nin puusi, ni#:- ‘Ti Ba, nyin bɛ la arizana ni, Kyɛ ka i yuuri du.
translated: The day that it happened, they said:- 'Our Father, we have come to greet you, God has brought us safely.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Kyɛ ka i naam kyɛ na; Kyɛ ka ba tumni i yuubu yɛla N‑niŋi duniya wa ni, Hoo ban niŋni arizana ni seem.
translated: Go and tell your chief; Go and tell them to prepare your hearts for My coming into the world, Because they will see wonderful things.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Dama ya yi kyɛ ya taaba tumbeeri taalli m‑pɛŋŋi ba, ya Ba ŋɔn bɛ arizana la gba nin kyɛ ya mammansi taalli m‑pɛŋŋi.
translated: And when you have turned again, strengthen your brethren, and He knows that you will deny Him three times.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Di fabinni beoo yɛla zuna, see ka i kyɛ ka beoo maa sa ya fabli ka maŋtoori yɛla. Zuna mun mari la ka toori yɛlimugsa ka a sakki.”
translated: They should not be afraid of people, but they should fear God who can destroy both body and soul in hell. People can only kill the body but cannot kill the soul.

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: Ni, “Ninvugyinni ku toaai n‑leebi daabri n‑ti dugmadima ayi: see ka u za'asi yinni yɛla ka wuri yinni la, bee u tibgi yinni la, ka laa n‑zeem ŋɔnla. Ya ku toaai n‑kyɛ ka Naawunni su ya yɛla ka kyɛ ka ligri gba su ya.”
translated: "They said, 'The elders have left us a proverb which says: if you see something and call it good or bad, or you touch it, it should not be in vain. We have seen and told God about it so that he may do something about it.'"

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: Dinzugu yan di fabinna, bee n‑teesiri, ni, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya di?’, bee, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya nyu?’, bee, ‘Bɔ ka ti ya yɛa?’
translated: Things that are forbidden, or we ask, 'Where did you go?', or, 'Where did you come from?', or, 'Where did you sleep?'

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: U daa laa m‑boosi ba, ni, “Ni wula ka i nya muŋŋa i mabii nimbiri ni ka zaantɔliga dee n‑sa i maŋtoori dinni ni ka i bu nya ka?
translated: They asked him, saying, "If you had a hundred sheep and one of them got lost, wouldn't you leave the ninety-nine on the mountain and go search for it?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Nyin birikɔnsi daana wa! Nyin daŋŋma n‑voaai i nimbiri ni zaantɔliga maa m‑basi ka nyari suŋŋa pɔi, ka naayi yiisi munseaa kan bɛ i mabii nimbiri ni!”
translated: You hypocrites! You know how to interpret the appearance of the earth and the sky, but why do you not know how to interpret this present time?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli, “Ya susima ka nin deaai yan susi seelli maa; ya booma ka nin nya yan boori seelli maa; ya kpaasima ka ba nin yoaai kyɛburu maa n‑ti ya.
translated: Jesus said to them, "Do not be anxious about what you will eat; do not worry about what you will drink; do not be troubled that they will not give you clothes to wear."

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tiseelli kam kan bu wanni walisuma, ba nin ŋmaai ka m‑basi, ka zaŋŋi ka n‑niŋi bugum ni.
translated: The elders said that if they don't get water, they will die of thirst, and they will never see tomorrow again.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Ka Yisa daa yɛli u, “Tɔ! N nin kyɛ i yiri maa ni na n‑tibi u.”
translated: And Jesus said to him, "Go! Your faith has healed you."

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Tɔ; ka Yisa ŋɔn daa wum toondaana maa ŋɔn daa yɛli seem maa ka di daa gyɛri u pam, ka u yɛli ban daa dɔli u maa, ni, “Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, halli Izraɛli dima ni gba n na zi n‑nya sɔsɔ ŋɔn niŋi yɛdda hoo doo wa ŋɔn niŋi seem!”
translated: But when Jesus saw the crowds and saw that they were harassed and helpless, and they were like sheep without a shepherd, he had compassion on them, saying, "The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few; pray therefore to the Lord of the harvest to send out laborers into his harvest!"

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Di saha ka ba daa tasi n‑yɛtta, “I ya niŋi ti la wula, nyin Naawunni Ŋɔn Ka Kpeaa biiya? I kyɛ kpa na ni i ti namsi ti, halli ka di saha maa na bu paai, bee?”
translated: They asked them saying, "Have you not heard that it is forbidden, that the Lord's Day is holy? Why do you do what you are not supposed to do, which our fathers did not do, right?"

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Tɔ: Ka ba daa du mɔ'ari maa duuri m‑paai Gadara dima tingbaŋŋu maa ni. M‑paai bɛni ka dɔppa ayi daa yi gbala zii maa na nti tuusi u, ka arizimbeeri daa daamni ba halli nti yakki, ka dizugu niriba daa bu laa n‑toaai n‑dɔli lala soori maa.
translated: When they arrived at the other side of the lake in the region of the Gadarenes, two men who had evil spirits came out from the tombs to meet him, and they were so fierce that no one dared to pass by that road.

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Ka Yisa daa yɛli ba, “Tɔ, ya kyɛma!”, ka arizimbeeri maa daa yi dɔppa maa ni n‑kyaŋŋi nti kpɛ kurikyuudima maa, ka ba zaa daa vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni n‑kpi.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "Come, follow me!", and immediately they left their nets and their fishing boats, and they all followed him with joy and gladness in their hearts.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Di saha ka Yisa daa laa n‑kpɛ nyariŋŋu maa ni n‑labi n‑kuli u toori tiŋŋa.
translated: They say that Jesus used to heal the sick and raise the dead to life again.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
text: Bee, ya teesim daam yɛla: ba mun ku laa n‑sakki n‑zaŋŋi dapaalli n‑lɛ daga'akura ni. Ba yi niŋi lala, ga'akura maa nin ŋma, ka daam maa yuuli m‑basi, ka ga'a maa gba sa'am. Ay! Ga'apaala ka ba zaŋŋni n leeri dapaalli n‑niŋni a ni, lala ka abayi maa zaa nin kpalim suŋŋa.”
translated: So, you should know this: they cannot just randomly choose leaders and elders. They must look carefully, the elder must be wise, the person must be mature and experienced, and the elder must have understanding. Yes! The elders that they choose to lead the community and look after it, they should not be people with hot tempers.

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Ka Yisa daa baŋŋi ban teesiri seem, ka daa yɛli ba, ni, “Wula ka ya teesiri teesibeeri?
translated: And Jesus asked them a question, saying to them, "Who do you say that I am?"

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: Ni, “Amaa m boori la ni ya baŋŋi, ni, ba pun ti man Ninsaala Biiya soori ni n kyɛri niriba taalli m‑pɛŋŋni ba, duniya wa puuni.” Di saha maa ka u ŋmaligi n‑yɛli baara maa, “N yɛtti i ni, ni, i doaama ka kpikki i bundoaa wa n‑kuli!”
translated: And, "But if I had known that they would do this, that they would take me to the Supreme Court and expose my secrets to people, the world would have ended." That is why he did not want to tell them, "I tell you that your friend has killed your child!"

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: Yisa ŋɔn daa kur yɛtti ba dinŋɔ maa, kpeensɔ daa kyɛ u saani na n‑gbaani u tooni ka yɛli u, “M bipumbila kpiya ni.” ni, “Amaa kyɛm na n‑zaŋŋi i nuusi m‑pa u zugu, ka u nin ti nya u nyɔvuri.”
translated: When Jesus had finished saying these things to them, the crowd was amazed at his teaching and said to him, "We have never seen anything like this," and, "But we want to see you do it again, so that we and our children may see your power."

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: ni Filipi, ni Baatɔlɔmiyu, ni Tɔmaasi ni Matiyu lampoo karikyi la; ni Alifiisi Gyeemsi, ni Taddiyusi,
translated: and Philip, and Bartholomew, and Thomas and Matthew the tax collector; and Alphaeus' James, and Thaddaeus,

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Dɔseeba nin gbaai ba mabiisi n‑tisi ka ba ku ba, bee ba nin gbaai ba biisi, ka biisi mun nin yisigi n‑zabi ba badima ni ba madima halli nti ku ba gba.
translated: The elders and their children should not forget to teach them, and they and their children should teach, so that the children will know and tell their grandchildren and their great-grandchildren until they all die.

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: Ka Yisa daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ŋɔnkam zaani niriba tooni n‑yɛli, ni, u nyɛ la man dinni, man gba nin ti zaani m Ba tooni arizana ni, n‑yɛli, ni, ŋɔn nyɛ la n dinni.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "Truly I say to you, whoever does not receive the kingdom of God like a child will never enter it."

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: Ni, “Ya di teesi, ni, m mari la nangbanyinni beesigu n‑kyɛ duniya wa na. Ay! M bu kyɛ na ni m mari nangbanyinni yɛla na, see zaba.
translated: He said, "You should know that I am the light of the world. Ah! I am not here as I was before, but for a short time.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: N kyɛ na ni n tugli la doowa ni u ba, ni bipuŋŋa ni u ma gba, ni bii pɔ'a ni u dinyaampɔ'a m‑paasi, ka ba zabi.
translated: If you see your neighbor's donkey or ox fallen down, do not ignore it, but help them lift it up.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Halli ŋɔn mun ku sakki n‑zaŋŋi u kum dɔpulli n‑dɔli n nyaaŋŋa, ŋɔn gba bu sumsi ni u bɛ n saani.
translated: The reason we are not able to do good things and live in peace is that we do not trust each other.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: Ka u daa laa n‑yɛli ba, “Ŋɔnkam zaa gyaari ya, mani ka u gyaari. Ka ŋɔnkam gyaari ma, Naawunni ka u gyaatira, dama ŋɔn Naawunni n tum ma na.
translated: If they do not listen to them, "Everyone will be judged, and you will be judged. If everyone is judged, God will judge you, because God's word is true.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Halli ŋɔnkam zaa mun yi ti zaŋŋi kɔmaasim n‑ti n nyaandɔlisɔ ni u ti nyu, ŋɔn nyɛ n nyaandɔla maa zugu, yɛlimaŋni ka n yɛtti ya wa, di daama gba ku toaai n‑kɔŋŋi u laara.”
translated: Even if all the others abandon you and run away, even if I am to die, I will never deny you, even if they kill me I will not leave your side.

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Gyɔni nyaandɔliba maa daa bakki n‑kyaŋŋi, ka Yisa mun daa piili n‑tɔ'asiri n‑ti daadam maa zaŋŋi n‑kyaŋŋi Gyɔni yɛla, ka boosiri ba, ni, “Bɔ ka ya daa kyaŋŋi mooni ni ya ti nya? Kyimnigu ka pɛbsigu zeera, bee?
translated: When John's disciples came and fasted, Jesus' disciples did not fast at all like John's people, and they asked him, saying, "Why don't your disciples fast like us? Is it a new teaching or what?"

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Di saha ka Gyɔni daa bɛ dansarika ni - Gyɔni sɔ ŋɔn daa su niriba Naawunni koom la. U daa ti wum Yisa ŋɔn daa tumni seem, ka daa tum u nyaandɔliba Yisa saani na,
translated: They did not believe in Jesus - Jesus who had come to save people from God's hand. They had heard what Jesus had been teaching, and had seen his miracles that Jesus did,

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Bee bɔ ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi ni ya ti nya? Ninvugsɔ ŋɔn bɔbi suŋŋa? Ya bu nya? - ninvugseeba ban yeeri bunveela n kur bɛ nadima yiya ni.”;
translated: Do they not see the birds above them spreading and folding their wings? What holds them up? You do not see? - truly those who believe in the resurrection and the Day of Judgment will understand.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: ka laa m‑boosi ba n‑yɛli, “Ka bɔ zugu ka ya daa lee n‑kyaŋŋi? - ni ya ti nya anabisɔ, bee? Yɛlimaŋni ka n ya yɛli ya, ni, halli u gaari anabi gba.
translated: and they asked them saying, "Why did you come to this place? - or what do you want, eh? Tell us what you want, or, until your car gets twelve."

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Ka man mun Ninsaala Bii ti kyɛ na ka man ni niriba la'amni n‑dira ka nyuura, ka ya yɛli, ni, ‘Ya bu nya doo wa ŋɔn nyɛ beem daana ni danyuura seem? - ka u laa n‑lɔ zoori ni lampodeaariba ni ala'akyi daandima.’ Amaa Naawunni yam tumni n‑wulisiri kamaŋŋa yɛla.”
translated: And when the Son of Man comes in his glory and all the angels with him, then he will sit on his glorious throne, and he will say to them, 'Why did you not give me food when I was hungry and water when I was thirsty? - and you did not visit me with the sick and the prisoners in jail.' But the Lord's word will be fulfilled completely.

ref: Haven't you read in the Law of Moses that the priests are allowed to work in the temple on the Sabbath? But no one says they are guilty of breaking the law of the Sabbath.
text: Bee, ya na zi n‑karim Musa zaaligu yɛla? Ni, maalimaanniba tumni la ba tuma Naawunni Gyeeŋŋu Yiri puuni Vuusim Dabsiri. Ban n tumni la Vuusim Dabsiri maa ka bu deaari taalli di zugu.
translated: So, do you know what Moses did? Yes, the teachers said that they will meet at the Church on Friday. They said that Friday is not a market day at all.

ref: Don't you know what the Scriptures mean when they say, “Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others?” If you knew what this means, you would not condemn these innocent disciples of mine.
text: Sɔppu la yɛliya, ni, ‘Man Naawunni bu boori ni ya maanni m‑maanni bunkɔbri wa, amaa m boori ya zɔri niriba namboo.’ Ya siri da mi dinŋɔ gbinni, ya naa n‑ku galim niriba ka ba bu niŋi taalli halli beela.
translated: The scripture says, 'I do not want the death of the wicked, but I want them to turn from their evil ways and live.' This shows us clearly that God does not want people to go to hell forever.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Dinŋɔ daa niŋi ni di kyɛ ka seelikam siri ti niŋi hoo anabi Aizaaya ŋɔn daa pun yɛli seem la: ni#:-
translated: The scripture that he was reading was this one that the prophet Isaiah had written:

ref: Some Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, we want you to show us a sign from heaven.”
text: Di saha maa ka karimba dimseeba daa tɔ'asi n‑ti u, ni Farisii dimseeba m‑paasi, n‑yɛli, ni, “Ti Karimba, ti boori la i tum alahazibu tunseaa n‑wulisi ti.”
translated: And the tax collectors and sinners were all drawing near to him, and the Pharisees and scribes murmured, saying, "This man receives sinners and eats with them."

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Ka u daa labsi n‑yɛli ba, ni, “Zuna niriba wa nyɛ la ninvugbeeri ban boori zina. Ba kur bɛ la alahazibu nyaabu ni, ni ba ti baŋŋi yɛla, amaa ba ku ti nya sɛseelli m‑paasi Anabi Gyoona alahazibu yɛla maa.
translated: And he answered them, saying, "An evil and adulterous generation seeks for a sign, but no sign will be given to it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.

ref: He was in the stomach of a big fish for three days and nights, just as the Son of Man will be deep in the earth for three days and nights.
text: Gyoona ŋɔn daa bɛ zintitaari maa puu ni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata maa, lala ka man Ninsaala Bii maa gba nin bɛ tingbaŋŋu puuni n‑niŋi daba ata ni yunsi ata bɛni.
translated: The reason why there is no development in our area or in our villages or towns, is because God has not given us leaders who have our area or villages or towns at heart.

ref: The Queen of the South will also stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something much greater than Solomon. Return of an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Pɔ'a-naaba sɔ ŋɔn daa su nudirigu luga tinseaa la daa yi wa'aligu n‑kyɛ na ni u ti wum NaaSulɛmaana yam yɛttɔ'a maa, ka dizugu u nin doaai n‑galim zuna niriba wa Zaadaari maa, dama ŋɔn gaari NaaSulɛmaana bɛ la kpa zuna ka ba bu ya wum.”
translated: The chief said that he had sent a message to the elders and they had gone to greet NaaSulɛmaana at his house, and he had told them to go and inform the people at Zaadaari, because it was NaaSulɛmaana who had the authority to give them permission to bury him.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, his mother and brothers came and stood outside because they wanted to talk with him.
text: Ka Yisa ŋɔn daa na tɔ'asiri n‑tiri zama maa, u ma ni u tunzɔppa daa paai na n‑zaani samanni, ni, ba boori n‑tɔ'asi yɛttɔ'aseaa n‑ti u.

translated: When they saw their neighbors and those who knew them before, they said, "Is this not the one who used to sit and beg?" Some said, "It is him." Others said, "No, he just looks like him." But he himself said, "I am the one." So they asked him, "How then were your eyes opened?"

translated: In the past they used to build their houses with mud, with grass roofs, both big and small. They were strong, and could withstand rain and sun on them.

text: Ka u daa ti ba soori, ka ba daa yi n‑kyaŋŋi n‑kpɛ kurikyuudima maa ni - kurikyuudima maa mun daa paai tusa ayi - ka ba daa vaai n‑guui halli nti lu mɔ'ari ni ka koom daa di ba.
translated: And they would go to their farms, and they would take their children to school - the school that was three miles away - and they would fetch water from the well and cook food for them.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Di saha maa gba ka pɔ'asɔ daa bɛ ba sunsukkuni, ka u daa soori halli yuuma piiya ni ayi ka ziim bu goaara.
translated: They should not be like their ancestors, a stubborn and rebellious generation, whose hearts were not loyal to God and whose spirits were not faithful to him.

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.

translated: But if you do not repent and turn away, it will destroy you.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Di saha ka pɔ'a maa daa gyigri ni dabeem ka mun mi din niŋi u seem, ka u daa kyɛ na nti gbaani Yisa tooni ka yɛli u din niŋi u maa zaa kasi.
translated: They wanted to bring the sick and those with various diseases to touch even the edge of his garment, and all who touched him were healed.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Yoomyooma ka bipumbila maa daa doaai n‑zaani n‑kyɛnni n‑gyinna: - u daa nyɛ la yuuma piiya ni ayi biiya. Nŋɔ maa daa niŋi ba alahazibu ni sufupeelli pam.
translated: In the past, our ancestors used to do three things: - they used to farm and raise animals. That is what gave them wealth and prosperity.

translated: And they answered and said to him, "We have nothing here except five loaves of bread and two fish."

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ka ba daa doaai n‑kpɛ ba nyariŋŋu la n‑kpa luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni, ba kɔkɔa.
translated: If they did not take care of their children and teach them good behavior, they would be punished.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Ka saha ŋɔn daa pun yuui ka yuŋŋu zibri maa, u nyaandɔliba maa daa kyɛ na nti yɛli u, “Saha pun gaari, ka kpa wa laa n‑nyɛ la luginseelli sɔ ŋɔn ka ni.
translated: When the man had finished speaking and had sat down, the leaders stood up and said to him, "Speak well, and show us the truth about what you are saying."

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Ka Yisa labsi n‑yɛli ba, “Ya tim ba bunseelli ka ba di.” Ka ba labsi m‑boosi u, ni, “Ammi, i boori la ti kyaŋŋi nti da ligri pam paanu na n‑diisi ba, bee? Ay! - ti ligri ku paai lala!”
translated: And Jesus answered them, "Let them go and buy food for themselves to eat." And they answered him, saying, "Should we go and buy bread worth two hundred denarii to give them to eat? Ah! - we don't have that much money!"

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: Di saha ka Yisa naayi yɛli u nyaandɔliba maa, ni, ba kyɛ ka niriba maa puri ba mansi n‑zinni mɔmaasa la zugu.
translated: They did not want Jesus to tell his disciples, so that they could catch the people and kill them in the darkness.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Di nyaaŋŋa, sahanseaa u mammaŋŋa ŋɔn daa kpalim n‑kyeoosiri niriba maa, Yisa daa kyɛ ka u nyaandɔliba kpɛ nyariŋŋu puuni la n‑daŋŋi tooni n‑lɔŋŋi mɔ'aduuri sa n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa ban boonni Bɛtsaida la.
translated: At that time, when the crowd had gathered again and there was nothing for them to eat, Jesus called his disciples and said to them, "I have compassion on the crowd because they have been with me for three days and have nothing to eat."

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Ka ŋɔn daa kyeoosi ba n‑naai, u daa kyaŋŋi n‑du zoori zugu ni u ti puusi Naawunni.
translated: If they had not believed in him, they would have turned back and not reached Canaan.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Ban daa yi nyariŋŋu maa ni la, bɛni niriba maa daa baŋŋi ya'abi, ni, Yisa n nyɛ u,
translated: When they saw the child, they told people what they had been told about Jesus,

text: Sɔkanŋɔ n nyɛ wulisiseesi yan mari n‑wulisiri seeba n‑kpiisiri Naawunni zaaligu. Ka yɛla pam anŋɔ taabu bɛ ni ka ya niŋna.”
translated: The reason is that the elders and those who teach the Word of God are not being respected. If you do this, there will be no problem and you will be blessed.

text: Seelli ka ni din nin kpɛ ninsaala puuni ka kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana Naawunni saani. Amaa bunni din yiiri nira puuni na n kyɛ ka u leebri da'ari daana.”
translated: The world and its inhabitants will pass away and disappear like smoke before the Lord comes again. But those who do God's will will live forever.

text: - Yɛlibeeri anŋɔ taabu zaa yiiri la ninsaala puuni na n‑kyɛ ka u leebi da'ari daana.”
translated: The reason for all this trouble is that people refuse to obey God's commandments.

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Yisa daa yisigi n‑yi bɛni n‑kyaŋŋi tinseaa kan pɔri Taaya tiŋŋa la. U daa kpɛ yiri ni ka bu boori ni sɔsɔ baŋŋi, ni, u bɛ bɛni. Amaa u daa bu nyaŋŋi n‑sɔ'ai.

translated: And Jesus said to him, "Go! Your faith has healed you." And immediately he received his sight and followed him on the road.

text: Ka Yisa daa zaŋŋi u n‑yi kpaŋŋa sa u kɔkɔ ka kyɛ zama maa, ka zaŋŋi u nubisi n‑tum doo maa tuba ni; ka tusi nantoori n‑zaŋŋi n‑siisi doo maa zulimni.
translated: And God saw that it was good in his eyes, and God separated the light from the darkness; and God called the light day and the darkness he called night.
